name: From Fuentes de Ropel to Virgen de la Vega
name_original: De Fuentes de Ropel a la Virgen de la Vega — circular por Benavente
description: This moderate 37.2-mile road cycling route from Fuentes de Ropel to Virgen de la Vega offers rural views and cultural landmarks.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Fuentes De Ropel traverse a landscape characterized by open fields, plains of cereal, and oak groves. The region is shaped by the presence of the Esla River, which routes often follow, providing varied terrain and scenic views. Cyclists encounter mostly well-paved surfaces, with routes ranging from moderate elevation gains to longer excursions. Best road cycling routes around Fuentes De Ropel The most popular road cycling route is Benavente: Tera and Esla River Valleys Loop, a 42.6 miles (68.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 51 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the Tera and Esla river valleys. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Benavente – tour around Fuentes de Ropel and río Esla – Villafer, a moderate 32.3 miles (52.0 km) path. This route showcases the plains and the Esla River near Fuentes de Ropel. Local road cyclists also love the Benavente and Villafáfila Lagoons Loop, a 63.7 miles (102.5 km) trail leading through open fields and near the Villafáfila Lagoons, often completed in about 4 hours 6 minutes. Road cycling around Fuentes De Ropel is defined by open fields, plains of cereal, and routes along the Esla River. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Fuentes De Ropel are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 5 reviews. More than 200 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Fuentes De Ropel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Fuentes de Ropel?
A: There are 29 road cycling routes around Fuentes de Ropel, offering a variety of options for different skill levels. The komoot community has rated these routes with an average score of 5.0 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Fuentes de Ropel?
A: The terrain around Fuentes de Ropel is characterized by open fields and plains of cereal, interspersed with oak groves. Many routes follow the course of the Esla River, providing varied and scenic views. You'll find mostly well-paved surfaces suitable for road cycling.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of road cycling routes in Fuentes de Ropel?
A: The routes cater to various fitness levels. There are 6 easy routes, 22 moderate routes, and 1 difficult route available, ensuring options for beginners to more experienced cyclists.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023215007/benavente-and-villafafila-lagoons-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benavente and Villafáfila Lagoons Loop&lt;/a&gt; cover over 60 miles (100 km), taking approximately 4 hours to complete and leading through open fields and near the Villafáfila Lagoons.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Fuentes de Ropel?
A: Many routes in the region are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1351326158/benavente-tera-and-esla-river-valleys-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benavente: Tera and Esla River Valleys Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023215007/benavente-and-villafafila-lagoons-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benavente and Villafáfila Lagoons Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features can I see while road cycling in Fuentes de Ropel?
A: You'll encounter the serene Esla River, which many routes follow, and the confluence of the Cea and Esla rivers. The landscape also features open fields, plains of cereal, and picturesque oak groves. Some routes pass near the Villafáfila and Villalpando Natural Reserve Lagoons, offering diverse natural scenery.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. You might pass by the medieval Church of San Pedro or the Church of Santa María de Arbás. Other historical remnants include a Roman bridge over the Cea River, traditional bodegas, dovecotes, and an old mill. The town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2720898" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benavente&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable settlement nearby with historical significance.

Q: Can I spot any wildlife while cycling in Fuentes de Ropel?
A: The plains of cereal and dehesas are important habitats for birds, including a significant population of great bustards and other nesting birds. This makes the area appealing for birdwatching opportunities while you cycle.

Q: Are the road cycling routes in Fuentes de Ropel mostly paved?
A: Yes, the road cycling routes in Fuentes de Ropel primarily feature well-paved surfaces, making them ideal for road bikes. The region is known for its maintained roads suitable for cycling.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in Fuentes de Ropel?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' routes are not explicitly detailed, the presence of 6 easy routes and generally well-paved surfaces suggests that some of the shorter, flatter loops could be suitable for families. Always check the route details for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Fuentes de Ropel?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the villages, including Fuentes de Ropel and nearby Benavente, which serve as common starting points for many routes. Look for designated parking areas within these settlements.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Fuentes de Ropel?
A: The region offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer and potential colder, wetter conditions in winter.

Q: Is public transport available to access road cycling routes in Fuentes de Ropel?
A: While Fuentes de Ropel is a smaller village, nearby larger towns like Benavente may offer public transport connections. It's advisable to check local bus schedules if you plan to use public transport to reach your starting point.
tips: Catholic church dedicated to the Virgen de la Vega, patron saint of the city of Benavente. After the Christian troops of Alfonso III defeated the Muslims in the battle of La Polvoraria that took place near Benavente in the year 878. The victory is celebrated every year in a festival that bears his name.
Plaza Mayor de Benavente that includes buildings such as the Benavente Town Hall and the Information and Tourism Office, banks, cafes and bars. Everything you may need on your trip.

A small town in the region of Benavente and Los Valles. The most notable building is that of San Pedro Apóstol, although it could be said that the most modern agricultural buildings that surround the landscape are more striking.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While classified as 'moderate' on komoot, this 59.9 km route is characterized by mostly well-paved surfaces, making it an easy ride for those with good fitness levels. It's a good choice for road bikes.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is circular, starting and ending in the vicinity of Fuentes de Ropel, taking you through Virgen de la Vega and Benavente.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is predominantly well-paved, making it ideal for road cycling. You can expect a smooth ride with an elevation gain of approximately 250 meters over the entire 59.9 km.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a blend of rural agricultural landscapes and local culture. You'll pass through Fuentes de Ropel, known for its church of San Pedro Apóstol, and connect to Virgen de la Vega, which has a Catholic church dedicated to the patron saint of Benavente. You'll also encounter Plaza Mayor de Benavente.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of the Camino de Santiago del Sureste and Camino Castropepe.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the route?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in the towns along the route, such as Fuentes de Ropel or Benavente, which serve as convenient starting points for this circular tour.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, and while dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, it's not typically considered a 'dog-friendly trail' in the same way a hiking path might be. If cycling with a dog, ensure it's safely secured in a trailer or basket, and always follow local regulations.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit to cycle in this area of Zamora province?
faqs.answer: Generally, no specific permits are required for recreational road cycling on public roads in the Zamora province. However, always adhere to Spanish traffic laws, including helmet use and proper lighting if cycling in low light conditions.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The route can be enjoyed for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, while summer can be warm but manageable, especially during early mornings. Winters are generally mild but check local weather conditions for rain or colder temperatures.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, Benavente, a significant town on the route, offers various amenities including restaurants and accommodation options. Smaller towns like Fuentes de Ropel may have more limited choices, but you can find cafes and pubs.

faqs.question: What kind of bike is best suited for this route?
faqs.answer: Given the predominantly well-paved surfaces, a racebike (road bike) is the ideal choice for this route, allowing for an efficient and enjoyable ride.
